headline: McCone County Created, 1919
body:
Well now, if you ever found yourself out amongst the wide-open spaces of eastern Montana, where the sky stretches out like a promise and the land rolls on forever, you might just stumble upon a place called McCone County. It ain't as old as some of them grizzled prospectors' tales, but its birth in 1919 was a right proper affair, a testament to the ceaseless itch of folks to tame the wild and carve out a piece of civilization, even if it meant a bit of legislative wrangling.

Back in them days, Montana was still a young pup, growing faster than a jackrabbit with a coyote on its tail. The big counties, like Dawson, were sprawling giants, and folks out in the far-flung corners felt about as connected to the county seat as a lone wolf to a city council meeting. They needed their own patch, their own say-so, their own place to hang their hats and settle their disputes. And so, the good citizens, with a keen eye for progress and a hankering for local governance, set about petitioning the powers-that-be in Helena.

It was on the twentieth day of February, in the year of our Lord nineteen hundred and nineteen, that the Montana State Legislature, after much deliberation and perhaps a fair bit of tobacco-chewing, gave its official nod. They took a generous slice of Dawson County, a piece of land as rich in promise as a gold strike, and declared it McCone County. They named it after a fellow named George McCone, a state senator who, by all accounts, was a man of some standing and had, in his earlier days, served as a commissioner in that very Dawson County they were now carving up. A fitting tribute, I reckon, to a man who understood the lay of the land and the spirit of its people.

This wasn't just some bureaucratic reshuffling, mind you. No, sir. This was the very pulse of progress, the steady beat of settlement echoing across the plains. Homesteaders, with dreams as big as the Montana sky, had been pouring into this territory, turning sod and raising families. Ranchers, with their herds spread wide, needed a closer hand to guide their affairs. And so, a new county was born, with its heart set in the town of Circle, a place that was fast becoming a bustling hub for all them agricultural endeavors, from dryland farming to the raising of cattle.

With the creation of McCone County came the promise of order and opportunity. Schools for the young 'uns, lawmen to keep the peace, and roads to connect one neighbor to another – all the trappings of a civilized society, delivered right to their doorstep. It was a grand undertaking, a reflection of that peculiar American gumption to always be building, always be moving forward, even if it meant drawing new lines on an old map. As one observer of the era might have put it, surveying the vast expanse of the newly formed county, "The land, it seems, is ever ready for a new chapter, and man, with his restless spirit, is ever ready to write it." And so, the story of McCone County began, a fresh page in the unfolding saga of Montana.
